content:
- Executed task 04 end-to-end after skeptical plan verification: replaced pginfo SELECT 1 with a one-query snapshot SQL, implemented typed poll mapping via tokio-postgres, WAL/timeline parsers, readiness/state derivation, and worker step publishing through state channels.
- Expanded PgInfoWorkerCtx with postgres_dsn/poll_interval/publisher, updated contract tests, and added pginfo unit + real PG16 integration tests (primary transition, WAL movement, slot projection, standby/replica mapping) with binary-gated skip behavior.
- Full required gates now pass sequentially: make check, make test, make test-bdd, make lint. Hit and resolved one transient linker/cache-contention failure when I briefly ran top-level make targets in parallel; documented this in AGENTS.md.
- Bookkeeping done: task file marked done/passing with checkboxes ticked, task_switch executed, next step is commit all modified files with the required task-finished message.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and confirmed there are many <passes>false</passes> tasks.
- Applied priority rules: priority-tagged tasks outrank untagged; ultra_high chain in story-rust-system-harness is currently blocked by high-priority prerequisites.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/05-task-dcs-worker-trust-cache-watch-member-publish.md as the next logical unblocked high-priority task to unlock task 08 -> 12 ultra_high path.
- Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stopped immediately per instructions.

- Loaded prior progress memory and current task pointer; current task is story-rust-system-harness/05-task-dcs-worker-trust-cache-watch-member-publish.md.
- Read DCS skeleton plus surrounding pginfo/state/watch/ha contracts and task docs to build an implementation-ready plan with explicit semantics and tests.
- Wrote a full execution plan into task 05 with detailed steps for keys parsing, trust evaluation, store/watch adapter boundaries, worker loop behavior, unit/integration tests, and required gate ordering.
- Added terminal marker `TO BE VERIFIED` at the end of the task file as requested; did not execute code changes beyond planning.
- Next step in following turn: read task 05, detect `TO BE VERIFIED`, perform deep skeptical verification, alter at least one plan item, then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Checked current lint setup for unwrap/expect enforcement.
- Found `make lint` runs `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` only; no explicit `clippy::unwrap_used`/`clippy::expect_used` lints configured in repo manifests/src attributes.
- Confirmed no `clippy.toml`, `.cargo/config`, or `clippy::`-specific deny/warn attributes in crate root; existing unwrap/expect calls remain in code and tests.
- Next step: if needed, add explicit `clippy::unwrap_used` and `clippy::expect_used` (with `-D` or crate-level `#![deny...]`) and rerun `make lint`.

- Used the add-task-as-agent skill to create a new task for strict Rust lint policy enforcement.
- Checked active task/story context and verified numeric slots 01..16 were occupied; inserted a high-queue free slot as 05a between tasks 05 and 06.
- Added task file `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/05a-task-enforce-strict-rust-lints-no-unwrap-expect-panic.md` with explicit scope for strict clippy config and banning unwrap/expect/panic patterns in runtime code.
- Should do next if asked: execute this task by wiring clippy deny rules in lint config + make target and then refactor existing runtime unwrap/expect usages to typed errors.
